signals.headline: South Korea 2026 GDP Growth Forecast Slashed to 1
signals.raw:
South Korea 2026 GDP Growth Forecast Slashed to 1.7% by OECD on Energy Risks | YES | MED confidence** [1]  
OECD downgraded South Korea's 2026 GDP growth from 2.1% to 1.7%, citing Middle East conflict and energy import dependence, with further cuts to 1% by Natixis and 1.6% by Capital Economics.[1][5] Korean government announced a 25 trillion won stimulus without added debt.[1]

signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 Inflation Rises to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 Inflation Rises to 2.2%, Below Forecasts | NO | HIGH confidence** [2]  
South Korea's annual inflation increased to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% prior, missing market expectations of 2.4%, with pressures contained near the Bank of Korea's 2% target despite energy volatility.[2] This actual data tempers stagflation fears amid Middle East risks.[1][2]
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: OECD Raises G20 2026 Inflation Forecast to 4% Amid Global Pressures | YES | MED confidence** [1]
signals.raw:
OECD Raises G20 2026 Inflation Forecast to 4% Amid Global Pressures | YES | MED confidence** [1]  
OECD's interim outlook lifted G20 consumer inflation to 4% for 2026 (up 1.2 points), with South Korea at 2.7% and Germany at 2.9%, linked to Middle East war impacts on energy.[1] J.P. Morgan sees stable global core CPI at 2.8%.[4]
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.raw:
